weather_report:
Cottesloe wakes to a polished blue, the kind of morning where light moves like silk across the water and the day already feels well defined. Skies are clean and unruffled, with the forecast promising the same clear dome through late morning into the early afternoon. Expect sharp visibility and a crisp, sunlit clarity that flatters every edge. The sun will have bite as it climbs, with the UV running high around midday; it’s the sort of day that glows beautifully and burns quietly if you let it.

Dress light and breathable, a brimmed hat and good sunglasses earning their keep, sunscreen as habitual as keys in your pocket. A gentle swim or a quick beach walk suits the hour, followed by a shaded coffee or a quiet errand on foot before the afternoon glare sets in. If you’ve laundry to dry, the line will reward you; if you’ve pages to read, find a tree and linger there. Save your longer wandering for later when the sun eases and the color of the day softens.
